name: Cranberry-Orange Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Sour milk (1/2 tablespoon lemon juice mixed with 1/4 cup fluid milk and a pinch of skim milk powder)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Spray at least 12 paper-lined muffin cups with non-stick cooking spray.
Measure 1/2 tablespoon lemon juice in a measuring cup, add about 1/4 cup fluid milk and mix in a pinch of skim milk powder until slightly thickened. Set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the dry ingredients: 3/4 cup natural bran, 3/4 cup flour, 1/2 cup sugar (or less for diabetic recipes), 1/2 teaspoon baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon baking soda.
Add the remaining ingredients to the dry mixture and stir until just combined. Do not overmix.
Divide the batter evenly among the prepared muffin cups. Bake for about 20 minutes or until lightly browned and firm to the touch. Adjust baking time if using smaller muffins.
VARIATIONS:
- Substitute cranberries with Banana-Date: Use 1 cup mashed banana and 1/2 cup dates or raisins. If needed, add a splash of concentrated orange juice or orange/pineapple.
- Apple-Raisin: Replace with 1 cup applesauce and 1/2 cup raisins.
- Zucchini: Substitute with 1 cup grated unpeeled zucchini and 1/2 cup raisins.
For diabetic recipes, reduce sugar to 1/4 cup or 1 teaspoon per serving (as recommended by the American Diabetes Association).
This recipe is a healthier option with reduced sodium, cholesterol, and fat, but slightly higher in sugar compared to typical diabetic recipes.
